result:
The scent of rain on dry earth always brings a wave of nostalgia. It reminds me of summer afternoons spent sprawled on the porch swing, listening to the rhythmic drumming on the tin roof. The world felt bigger then, filled with the boundless possibilities of childhood. Now, smelling that same scent conjures a different kind of longing – a yearning for the simplicity and freedom of those days. 

Lately, I've been feeling the tug of that feeling more strongly. Maybe it’s the constant stream of news, the anxieties of modern life, or simply the passage of time. Whatever the cause, I find myself craving stillness. I want to disconnect from the digital noise and reconnect with the quiet spaces within myself. Perhaps that’s why I've started reading poetry again. Words woven with such care, capable of capturing the ephemeral things – a fleeting emotion, the glint of sunlight on water, the unspoken language of nature. It feels like a balm, a way to gently soothe the rough edges of existence. And, of course, a fresh cup of coffee and a good book go hand-in-hand with that feeling too!

The world felt bigger then, filled with the boundless possibilities of childhood. Now, smelling that same scent conjures a different kind of longing – a yearning for the simplicity and freedom of those days. 

Lately, I've been feeling the tug of that feeling more strongly. Maybe it’s the constant stream of news, the anxieties of modern life, or simply the passage of time. Whatever the cause, I find myself craving stillness. I want to disconnect from the digital noise and reconnect with the quiet spaces within myself. Perhaps that’s why I've started reading poetry again.
